---
title: "Genre Decoded"
execute:
  echo: false
format:
  html:
    theme: none
    css: styles.css
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

<div class="hero">
  <div class="hero-label">A Spotify Data Story</div>
  <h1>Genres have <em>personalities</em> — but not as different as you'd think.</h1>
  <p class="hero-sub">
    We analysed 28,000 songs across 6 genres using Spotify's audio fingerprints.
    Here's what we found.
  </p>
  <div class="scroll-hint">Scroll to explore</div>
</div>

<div class="section-divider"></div>

<section class="scroll-section" id="genre-personality">
  <div class="scrolly" id="genre-scrolly">
    <div class="scrolly-steps" id="genre-steps">
      <div class="step is-active" data-step="intro">
        <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Genre Attributes</div>
        <h2>What makes a genre a genre?</h2>
        <p>Spotify measures every song on six dimensions: how danceable it is,
        how much energy it carries, how happy it sounds, how acoustic it feels,
        how fast it moves, and how speech-heavy it is.</p>
        <p style="margin-top:0.75rem">Together, these form a <strong>sonic fingerprint</strong>.
        The radar chart shows the median fingerprint for all six genres at once.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="step" data-step="latin-edm">
        <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Latin vs EDM</div>
        <h2>The happiest genre isn't EDM — it's Latin.</h2>
        <p><strong>Latin</strong> scores highest on both <em>valence</em> (happiness)
        and <em>danceability</em>. EDM is the most energetic, but sits surprisingly
        low on valence — intense, not joyful.</p>
        <div class="takeaway"><strong>Try it:</strong> Click <em>latin</em> and <em>edm</em>
        in the legend to isolate them and compare their radar shapes.</div>
      </div>
      <div class="step" data-step="rap">
        <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Rap</div>
        <h2>Rap has one feature that sets it apart from everything else.</h2>
        <p>Speechiness — the measure of how many words are in a track — spikes
        hard for rap. Every other genre clusters near zero.</p>
        <div class="takeaway"><strong>Try it:</strong> Click <em>Speechiness</em>
        in the feature buttons to highlight it in the bar chart.</div>
      </div>
      <div class="step" data-step="pop">
        <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Pop</div>
        <h2>Pop is the genre of averages.</h2>
        <p>On every feature, pop sits closest to the dataset mean. No defining spike,
        no extreme score. Pop's commercial logic is to borrow from everywhere and offend no one.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="step" data-step="similarity">
        <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Takeaway</div>
        <h2>No genre is truly extreme. The gaps are smaller than you'd expect.</h2>
        <p>The radar polygons overlap significantly. Latin and R&amp;B are the most
        similar pair. EDM and R&amp;B are the most different.</p>
        <div class="takeaway"><strong>Next:</strong> If genres are already converging
        in sound, how much further have they come over the decades?</div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="scrolly-sticky">
      <div class="chart-panel" id="main-chart-panel">
        <!-- OJS outputs are injected here by JS after render -->
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>
</section>

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
genreProfiles = FileAttachment("data/processed/genre_profiles.json").json()
topSongs      = FileAttachment("data/processed/top_songs.json").json()
FEATURES = ["Danceability","Energy","Valence","Acousticness","Tempo","Speechiness"]
```

```{ojs}
// Legend — OJS returns this node directly, no getElementById needed
viewof activeGenres = {
  const allGenres = genreProfiles.map(d => d.genre)
  const state = new Set(allGenres)

  const wrap = document.createElement("div")
  wrap.className = "genre-legend"
  wrap.value = state

  genreProfiles.forEach(d => {
    const item = document.createElement("div")
    item.className = "legend-item active"
    item.style.borderColor = d.color

    const dot = document.createElement("div")
    dot.className = "legend-dot"
    dot.style.background = d.color

    const label = document.createElement("span")
    label.textContent = d.genre.toUpperCase()

    item.append(dot, label)
    item.addEventListener("click", () => {
      if (state.has(d.genre)) {
        state.delete(d.genre)
        item.classList.remove("active")
        item.style.borderColor = "transparent"
        dot.style.opacity = "0.3"
      } else {
        state.add(d.genre)
        item.classList.add("active")
        item.style.borderColor = d.color
        dot.style.opacity = "1"
      }
      wrap.value = new Set(state)
      wrap.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }))
    })
    wrap.appendChild(item)
  })

  return wrap
}
```

```{ojs}
// Feature filter buttons
viewof activeFeature = {
  const wrap = document.createElement("div")
  wrap.className = "filters-wrap"
  wrap.value = null

  const lbl = document.createElement("div")
  lbl.className = "filters-label"
  lbl.textContent = "Highlight a feature →"

  const row = document.createElement("div")
  row.className = "feature-filters"

  let activeBtn = null
  FEATURES.forEach(feat => {
    const btn = document.createElement("button")
    btn.className = "filter-btn"
    btn.textContent = feat
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
      if (activeBtn === btn) {
        btn.classList.remove("active")
        activeBtn = null
        wrap.value = null
      } else {
        if (activeBtn) activeBtn.classList.remove("active")
        btn.classList.add("active")
        activeBtn = btn
        wrap.value = feat
      }
      wrap.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }))
    })
    row.appendChild(btn)
  })

  wrap.append(lbl, row)
  return wrap
}
```

```{ojs}
// Radar chart — returns an SVG wrapped in a div
radarViz = {
  const W = 260, H = 260
  const cx = W/2, cy = H/2, R = W*0.33
  const N = FEATURES.length
  const angles = FEATURES.map((_, i) => Math.PI*2*i/N - Math.PI/2)

  const polyPts = pairs => pairs.map(p => p.join(",")).join(" ")
  const pt = (i, v) => [cx + v*R*Math.cos(angles[i]), cy + v*R*Math.sin(angles[i])]

  const ns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  const svg = document.createElementNS(ns, "svg")
  svg.setAttribute("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
  svg.setAttribute("width", "100%")
  svg.style.overflow = "visible"

  // Grid rings
  ;[0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1].forEach(r => {
    const ring = document.createElementNS(ns, "polygon")
    ring.setAttribute("points", polyPts(angles.map(a =>
      [cx + r*R*Math.cos(a), cy + r*R*Math.sin(a)])))
    ring.setAttribute("fill","none")
    ring.setAttribute("stroke","#2a2a2a")
    ring.setAttribute("stroke-width","1")
    svg.appendChild(ring)
  })

  // Axes + labels
  FEATURES.forEach((feat, i) => {
    const a = angles[i]
    const ln = document.createElementNS(ns, "line")
    ln.setAttribute("x1", cx); ln.setAttribute("y1", cy)
    ln.setAttribute("x2", cx + R*Math.cos(a))
    ln.setAttribute("y2", cy + R*Math.sin(a))
    ln.setAttribute("stroke","#2a2a2a"); ln.setAttribute("stroke-width","1")
    svg.appendChild(ln)

    const tx = document.createElementNS(ns, "text")
    tx.setAttribute("x", cx + (R+20)*Math.cos(a))
    tx.setAttribute("y", cy + (R+20)*Math.sin(a))
    tx.setAttribute("text-anchor", Math.cos(a)>0.1?"start":Math.cos(a)<-0.1?"end":"middle")
    tx.setAttribute("dominant-baseline", Math.sin(a)>0.1?"hanging":Math.sin(a)<-0.1?"auto":"middle")
    tx.setAttribute("fill", activeFeature===feat ? "#f0f0f0" : "#555")
    tx.setAttribute("font-size","9")
    tx.setAttribute("font-family","DM Sans,sans-serif")
    tx.setAttribute("font-weight", activeFeature===feat ? "700" : "400")
    tx.textContent = feat.toUpperCase()
    svg.appendChild(tx)
  })

  // Genre polygons
  genreProfiles.forEach(d => {
    const isOn = activeGenres.has(d.genre)
    const poly = document.createElementNS(ns, "polygon")
    poly.setAttribute("points", polyPts(FEATURES.map((_,i) =>
      pt(i, d.features[FEATURES[i]] ?? 0))))
    poly.setAttribute("fill", d.color)
    poly.setAttribute("fill-opacity", isOn ? "0.15" : "0.02")
    poly.setAttribute("stroke", d.color)
    poly.setAttribute("stroke-width", isOn ? "2.5" : "0.5")
    poly.setAttribute("stroke-opacity", isOn ? "0.9" : "0.15")
    poly.style.transition = "all 0.3s ease"
    svg.appendChild(poly)
  })

  const wrap = document.createElement("div")
  const ttl = document.createElement("div")
  ttl.className = "chart-title"
  ttl.textContent = "Radar — Audio Fingerprints"
  wrap.append(ttl, svg)
  return wrap
}
```

```{ojs}
// Bar chart
barViz = {
  const W = 260, H = 200
  const m = {top:10, right:8, bottom:34, left:26}
  const iW = W-m.left-m.right, iH = H-m.top-m.bottom

  const ns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  const svg = document.createElementNS(ns, "svg")
  svg.setAttribute("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
  svg.setAttribute("width", "100%")

  const g = document.createElementNS(ns, "g")
  g.setAttribute("transform", `translate(${m.left},${m.top})`)
  svg.appendChild(g)

  // Grid + y-ticks
  ;[0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1].forEach(v => {
    const y = iH - v*iH
    const gl = document.createElementNS(ns,"line")
    gl.setAttribute("x1",0); gl.setAttribute("y1",y)
    gl.setAttribute("x2",iW); gl.setAttribute("y2",y)
    gl.setAttribute("stroke","#1e1e1e"); gl.setAttribute("stroke-width","1")
    g.appendChild(gl)

    const tk = document.createElementNS(ns,"text")
    tk.setAttribute("x",-4); tk.setAttribute("y",y)
    tk.setAttribute("text-anchor","end")
    tk.setAttribute("dominant-baseline","middle")
    tk.setAttribute("font-size","7")
    tk.setAttribute("font-family","DM Sans,sans-serif")
    tk.setAttribute("fill","#444")
    tk.textContent = v.toFixed(2)
    g.appendChild(tk)
  })

  const featW = iW / FEATURES.length
  const genreW = (featW - 2) / genreProfiles.length

  FEATURES.forEach((feat, fi) => {
    const isHl = activeFeature === feat || activeFeature === null

    const xl = document.createElementNS(ns,"text")
    xl.setAttribute("x", fi*featW + featW/2)
    xl.setAttribute("y", iH+13)
    xl.setAttribute("text-anchor","middle")
    xl.setAttribute("font-size","7.5")
    xl.setAttribute("font-family","DM Sans,sans-serif")
    xl.setAttribute("fill", activeFeature===feat ? "#f0f0f0" : "#444")
    xl.setAttribute("font-weight", activeFeature===feat ? "700" : "400")
    xl.textContent = feat.slice(0,5).toUpperCase()
    g.appendChild(xl)

    genreProfiles.forEach((d, gi) => {
      const isOn = activeGenres.has(d.genre)
      const val = d.features[feat] ?? 0
      const barH = val * iH

      const rect = document.createElementNS(ns,"rect")
      rect.setAttribute("x", fi*featW + 1 + gi*genreW)
      rect.setAttribute("y", iH - barH)
      rect.setAttribute("width", Math.max(genreW-1, 1))
      rect.setAttribute("height", barH)
      rect.setAttribute("fill", d.color)
      rect.setAttribute("opacity", isHl && isOn ? 0.85 : 0.07)
      rect.style.transition = "opacity 0.3s ease"

      rect.addEventListener("mouseenter", e => {
        let tip = document.querySelector(".chart-tooltip")
        if (!tip) { tip = document.createElement("div"); tip.className="chart-tooltip"; document.body.appendChild(tip) }
        tip.innerHTML = `<strong style="color:${d.color}">${d.genre.toUpperCase()}</strong><br>${feat}: ${val.toFixed(3)}`
        tip.classList.add("visible")
      })
      rect.addEventListener("mousemove", e => {
        const tip = document.querySelector(".chart-tooltip")
        if (tip) { tip.style.left=(e.clientX+14)+"px"; tip.style.top=(e.clientY-32)+"px" }
      })
      rect.addEventListener("mouseleave", () =>
        document.querySelector(".chart-tooltip")?.classList.remove("visible"))

      g.appendChild(rect)
    })
  })

  const wrap = document.createElement("div")
  const ttl = document.createElement("div")
  ttl.className = "chart-title"
  ttl.textContent = "Bar Chart — Feature Comparison"
  wrap.append(ttl, svg)
  return wrap
}
```

```{ojs}
// Song cards (shown only when exactly 1 genre is active)
songViz = {
  const active = [...activeGenres]
  if (active.length === 0 || active.length === genreProfiles.length)
    return document.createElement("div")

  const focusGenre = active[0]
  const color = genreProfiles.find(d => d.genre === focusGenre)?.color ?? "#888"
  const songs = topSongs.filter(s => s.genre === focusGenre).slice(0, 3)
  if (!songs.length) return document.createElement("div")

  const wrap = document.createElement("div")
  const ttl = document.createElement("div")
  ttl.className = "chart-title"
  ttl.textContent = `Top songs · ${focusGenre.toUpperCase()}`

  const cards = document.createElement("div")
  cards.className = "song-cards"
  songs.forEach((song, i) => {
    cards.innerHTML += `
      <div class="song-card" style="border-color:${color}33">
        <div class="song-card-rank" style="color:${color}">${i+1}</div>
        <div class="song-card-info">
          <div class="song-card-name">${song.track_name}</div>
          <div class="song-card-artist">${song.track_artist}</div>
        </div>
        <div class="song-card-pop">⬆ ${song.popularity}</div>
      </div>`
  })
  wrap.append(ttl, cards)
  return wrap
}
```

```{=html}
<style>
  /* Hide the OJS cell wrappers that appear OUTSIDE chart-panel */
  /* We move their children into chart-panel via JS below     */
  .ojs-in-panel { display: none; }
</style>

<script type="module">
// Wait for OJS to finish rendering all cells, then move the
// produced DOM nodes into the chart-panel grid.
// OJS cells with visible output produce a div.observablehq inside
// div[id^="ojs-cell-"] wrappers that sit inside #quarto-content.
// We relocate those nodes into the chart-panel.

function buildPanel() {
  const panel = document.getElementById("main-chart-panel")
  if (!panel) { setTimeout(buildPanel, 50); return }

  // The 5 visible OJS cells produce nodes that contain these classes:
  const legendNode  = document.querySelector(".genre-legend")
  const filtersNode = document.querySelector(".filters-wrap")
  const radarNode   = document.querySelector(".chart-title")  // first chart-title = radar
  const barNode     = document.querySelectorAll(".chart-title")[1]  // second = bar
  const songsNode   = document.querySelector(".song-cards")

  // We need the actual OJS wrapper divs, not just the inner nodes
  function ojsParent(el) {
    if (!el) return null
    let node = el
    // Walk up until we find a direct child of #quarto-content or main
    while (node && node.parentElement &&
           node.parentElement.id !== "quarto-content" &&
           node.parentElement.tagName !== "MAIN" &&
           !node.parentElement.classList.contains("observablehq-center")) {
      node = node.parentElement
    }
    return node
  }

  // Need all five before we proceed
  if (!legendNode || !filtersNode || !radarNode || !barNode) {
    setTimeout(buildPanel, 80)
    return
  }

  // Clear the panel placeholder text
  panel.innerHTML = ""

  // Build the 5 slot divs programmatically and move OJS output into them
  const slots = [
    { id: "slot-legend",  col: "1/-1", node: ojsParent(legendNode) },
    { id: "slot-filters", col: "1/-1", node: ojsParent(filtersNode) },
    { id: "slot-radar",   col: "1",    node: ojsParent(radarNode) },
    { id: "slot-bar",     col: "2",    node: ojsParent(barNode) },
  ]

  if (songsNode) {
    slots.push({ id: "slot-songs", col: "1/-1", node: ojsParent(songsNode) })
  }

  slots.forEach(({ id, col, node }) => {
    const slot = document.createElement("div")
    slot.id = id
    slot.style.gridColumn = col
    if (node) slot.appendChild(node)
    panel.appendChild(slot)
  })

  console.log("Chart panel built.")
}

// OJS needs some time to resolve FileAttachments and render cells
setTimeout(buildPanel, 600)
</script>

<script type="module">
import scrollama from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/scrollama@3.2.0/+esm"

function initScroller() {
  const steps = document.querySelectorAll("#genre-steps .step")
  if (!steps.length) { setTimeout(initScroller, 150); return }
  const s = scrollama()
  s.setup({ step: "#genre-steps .step", offset: 0.5 })
   .onStepEnter(({ element }) => {
     steps.forEach(el => el.classList.remove("is-active"))
     element.classList.add("is-active")
   })
  window.addEventListener("resize", s.resize)
}
initScroller()
</script>

<div class="chart-tooltip"></div>
```
